cause_analysis.narrative: lashedspa churned on 2026-02-18 after 266 days with Zoca. They sent an explicit cancellation request on 2026-01-06 via app_chat — 43 days before churn. Classification: explicit cancellation. The client formally asked to cancel; the question is whether a save attempt was made. 1 inbound message(s) went unanswered for 5+ days — this is the clearest operational failure on Zoca's side.
recommendations: [Retention] Cancellation was signalled 43 day(s) before churn via app_chat. Trigger an AM save call within 4 hours of any cancellation keyword — not a support response.
[Offer] Build a 3-tier retention offer ladder (pause / discount / scope change) so AMs have something concrete to counter with.
[SLA] 1 inbound message(s) went unanswered for 5+ days. Enforce a hard rule: every inbound gets a human response within 24h, every day of the week.
[SLA example] On 2026-02-24 via app_chat, client wrote: "I’m still getting notifications when my Zoca membership has been canceled?…" — no outbound response in 53 days.

messages.body: Hey Elaina - every missed call is a client walking away, and spam just eats your time. Zoca's AI receptionist answers every call, filters spam, and books on the line.
